There is an issue here where if the whole test times out after 10 minutes, the test won't be considered a failure, since 0 iterations finished:

    WARN[0630] No script iterations fully finished, consider making the test duration longer

I think in this mixed-load test you aim to run different scenarios concurrently.

However, in this setup, seems you run them sequentially by using executor: 'constant-vus'. As per documentation, the executor should be a type of: executor: 'shared-iterations' for a scenario e.g. 50 VUs for passthrough_read and 25 VUs for id_search_modue, and 25 VUs for cm_search_module.

I suggest to have a look at 'Distribute workloads across VUs' from official web site docs: https://grafana.com/docs/k6/latest/examples/distribute-workloads/
I measure this value "avg=2.93s" on my local, suggesting to set it 3000, otherwise may fail.
i measure this value "p(95)=19.56s" on my local run, suggesting to set i 20000
The scenarios do run in parallel even with constant-vus
There is separate task to adjust figures later: https://jira.onap.org/browse/CPS-2208

For now I've changed these so the pipeline will pass. But there is another task to adjust them later https://jira.onap.org/browse/CPS-2208
In future, we will base them on agreed requirements here: https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/CPS-391Spike%3A+Define+and+Agree+NCMP+REST+Interface#CPS391Spike:DefineandAgreeNCMPRESTInterface-Characteristics-WIP

import { sleep } from 'k6';
import http from 'k6/http';
import { check } from 'k6';
import { check } from 'k6';
import { check } from 'k6';

# Function to run k6 test scripts
run_tests() {
    local number_of_failures=0
    for test_script in "${ALL_TEST_SCRIPTS[@]}"; do
        echo "Running test: $test_script"
        k6 run --quiet "$test_script"
        local exit_code=$?
        if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
            echo "Test $test_script failed with exit code $exit_code"
            ((number_of_failures++))
        else
            echo "Test $test_script completed successfully"
        fi
        echo
    done
    return $number_of_failures
}

# Run tests
pushd "$(dirname "$0")" || exit 1
run_tests
popd || exit 1

# Print total number of failures
echo "NCMP TEST FAILURES: $?"

We should not log, as it will clutter the output.
k6 already reports summary of this:

         ✗ status equals 200
          ↳  24% — ✓ 49 / ✗ 151

In addition, if even a single check fails, k6 will return with error code:

    ERRO[0001] thresholds on metrics 'http_req_failed' have been crossed 

Also CPS logs are printed at the end, so failure cause can be investigated.

I'm against this detailed logging. These are not functional tests, just performance. We're only interested in average response time, throughput, etc. If there are failures, they will be shown in the summary, and can be investigated from CPS logs.

Logging every response would produce millions of lines of output.
For example, a single 30-second test of module ID search produces over 300MB of output. For all tests combined, it would be gigabytes of log output.
